contract_conformance.notes: Every contract surface matches exactly. Lane A cityFaces.ts exports planarFaces(graph)->CityFace[] and buildZonesFromFaces(faces,seed,zoneForCentroid)->BuildZone[] with the ZoneRule/CityFace/BuildZone interfaces verbatim; rules is always [] (cityFaces.ts:423). Circular-import discipline honored: CityInstance/CityZone and CityGraph are import-type only, the only runtime import is hashUnit from worldSpace, and the zone-hint derivation is an injected callback (cityFaces.ts:41-43, 404-408). planarFaces guard mirrors roadTileSetFrom EXACTLY (cityFaces.ts:220-227); node coords read x/z, edge endpoints read a/b. Lane B: hasRealGraph signature+semantics match (cityLayout.ts:379-386), computeCityPlan gains opts?:{graphLots?:boolean} (cityLayout.ts:892-895), CityPlan.zones?:BuildZone[] is additive and present ONLY on the graph-lots path via the spread (cityLayout.ts:1104), and the branch derives blockLots/emptyLots/blocks from zones while reusing realLots/roads/landmarks/streets/pedestrianTiles/extent unchanged and reusing assignBuildingLots as-is (cityLayout.ts:955-987). CityScape.tsx exports GRAPH_LOTS_ENABLED=false threaded into computeCityPlan with no new render component (CityScape.tsx:358-361, 572). Pillar 4 (no core special-casing) holds: the pentagon core is just another positive-area face.

coverage.notes: The full contract section 2 matrix is pinned in cityFaces.test.ts (16 tests): 5x5 grid -> 25 faces, pentagon -> 6 faces (5 outer + 1 core), dangling stub -> no spurious face, disconnected components -> every enclosing region kept, concave/near-collinear kept as loose polygon with lots clipped inside, thin sliver kept, empty/corrupt/no-edge -> [], and determinism (planarFaces + buildZonesFromFaces deep-equal AND JSON.stringify-identical under a node/edge shuffle). Contract section 3 wiring tests appended to cityLayout.test.ts (9 EM-264 tests, all green): hasRealGraph guard, off-path byte-identical (no-opts and {graphLots:false} both equal the classic_grid no-graph baseline; zones key absent off-path), graphLots:true with no real graph falls back byte-identical, on-path determinism + shuffle-invariance, pentagon -> 6 zones with non-empty lots, the flag actually fires (graphLots differs from grid, realLots keys shared), and citySignature reactivity (mutation changes sig, idle poll does not). Lot pads asserted inside their face polygon and seed proven load-bearing. The only untested layer is the live visual render, which is an explicit deferred USER sign-off gate by design (mirrors ROAD_MESH_ENABLED), not a coverage gap.

security.notes: Frontend-only, pure deterministic geometry. No I/O, no network, no eval, no dynamic import, no user-string interpolation into a sink, no injection surface. planarFaces is hardened against hostile/corrupt input: type-guarded nodes (finite x/z), dropped self-loops and dangling endpoints, a paranoia step bound so next() can never spin, and a ModelBoundary guard returning [] on non-array nodes/edges or no edges -- it never throws (a throw upstream of the per-piece ModelBoundary would crash the whole 3D world). buildZonesFromFaces skips malformed faces rather than throwing. MAX_LOTS_PER_FACE=256 caps per-face pad emission so a huge or degenerate face cannot explode the plan (DoS guard). No secrets, no credentials, no auth surface touched.

regression.notes: 0 net-new failures attributable to EM-264. Full run: 614 passed / 3 failed of 617 (vitest run src/components/world3d/), and tsc -b --force exits 0 (clean typecheck). cityFaces.test.ts (16) and cityLayout.test.ts (76, incl. the 9 appended EM-264 tests) are fully green. The 3 failures are ALL in CityScape.test.tsx and ALL driven by ROAD_MESH_ENABLED===true (the EM-247 temp flag, commit fa79ab4): the flag-default assertion, the road-tiles-vs-mesh render-path assertion, and the instancedMesh-count smoke -- none touch GRAPH_LOTS_ENABLED, cityFaces, or the graph-lots branch. These are the known pre-existing failures called out in the gate brief (verified by stash), not an EM-264 regression. The EM-155 byte-identical law is re-proven on-path: off-path JSON.stringify equals the no-graph baseline and the additive zones key is absent off-path.

failures_all_preexisting_road_mesh: CityScape.test.tsx > render smoke > mounts one instancedMesh per (key x chunk x part) (ROAD_MESH_ENABLED=true changes the road render path: 9 vs expected 16)
CityScape.test.tsx > ROAD_MESH_ENABLED flag > defaults to false (asserts false, flag is true)
CityScape.test.tsx > ROAD_MESH_ENABLED flag > flag off => road TILES render unchanged (0 tiles vs expected 4: mesh path active)

issues: INFO: 3 pre-existing failures in web/src/components/world3d/CityScape.test.tsx are caused by ROAD_MESH_ENABLED===true (EM-247 temp flag, commit fa79ab4), NOT by EM-264. They are unrelated to GRAPH_LOTS_ENABLED and out of this lane's file ownership. No action for EM-264; they clear when the EM-247 flag is reverted or its tests are updated post visual sign-off.

INFO (deferred, by design): GRAPH_LOTS_ENABLED ships OFF. The live visual sign-off (flip to true against a pentagon, confirm buildings land inside road-enclosed blocks, no grid-on-pentagon) is the deferred USER gate per contract section 5.6, mirroring the ROAD_MESH_ENABLED pattern -- not gateable here.

LOW: cityFaces.ts duplicates a local point-in-polygon / signed-area / centroid geometry kit and a LOT_PITCH=2.6 constant kin to cityLayout.TILE; this is a deliberate, contract-sanctioned choice to avoid the cityLayout import cycle, but is a candidate for a future shared geometry/worldSpace module (no functional impact).

gate_decision.reason: PASS. Contract conformance 5 (>=3) and security 5 (>=3) clear the gate thresholds; no CRITICAL/HIGH blocker. tsc -b --force clean (exit 0). 0 net-new EM-264 failures: cityFaces.test.ts (16) and cityLayout.test.ts (76, incl. 9 EM-264 tests) fully green; the only 3 failures are the known pre-existing ROAD_MESH_ENABLED (EM-247) cases, out of this lane's scope. The EM-155 off-path byte-identical law and EM-155 determinism (shuffle-invariance) are both re-proven, the zones key is additive/off-path-absent, and the pillar-2 never-throws/never-drops-a-region defenses are exercised across stub/disconnected/concave/corrupt graphs. Ships behind GRAPH_LOTS_ENABLED=false pending the deferred USER visual sign-off.
